_comment: Universal pulse: active probes of every critical HME element. Failure writes one-line entry to log/hme-errors.log via LIFESAVER so the next turn sees it immediately -- not 48 minutes later. Streak-based to suppress transient flakes.

process_probes.name: worker
process_probes._why: 48-min GIL hang was invisible because worker stayed ALIVE while one thread pegged CPU. Kill the invisibility: alert when a tracked process holds >=90% CPU for >=90s.

process_probes.name: proxy
process_probes._why: Proxy is node; a runaway event loop (or middleware stuck in a tight sync loop) shows as sustained high CPU.

file_freshness_probes.name: hook_latency_log
file_freshness_probes._why: Every hook writes to this log on exit via _safety.sh's EXIT trap. paired_path gates the alert: if BOTH path AND paired_path are equally stale, the session is just idle (not broken). The probe fires only when paired_path was updated within max_stale_sec but path was NOT -- that signals tool calls happening without hooks logging, which is the genuine 'hooks aren't firing' failure mode.

file_freshness_probes.name: hme_activity
file_freshness_probes._why: Activity bridge writes here for hook/proxy/fs events. Stale-for-30min means the bridge is idle or stopped.

file_freshness_probes.name: doctor_health
file_freshness_probes._why: hme-doctor writes this after successful health checks; stale means doctor stopped running or health is failing.

hook_latency_probes.name: all_hooks_p95
hook_latency_probes._why: Every hook self-logs wall-time to this JSONL on exit (via _safety.sh EXIT trap). p95 > 500ms over the last 10 min (min 10 samples) means turn-end and tool-call flow is visibly slowing. Previously captured but never alerted on.
hook_latency_probes._threshold_notes: Per-hook overrides -- `stop` legitimately runs ~600-700ms (autocommit + detectors run_all + /audit curl + lifesaver scan); 900ms allows normal variance without false alarms. sessionstart includes RAG-engine cold-start context-meter + holograph snapshot; 2.5s budget is realistic. userpromptsubmit includes LIFESAVER scan + onboarding checks; 700ms. stop_chain:holograph spawns `timeout 2 python3 snapshot-holograph.py --diff` -- p95 legitimately approaches the 2s hard cap; 2200ms = 2s timeout + 200ms shell-stage headroom. Lookup in universal_pulse_tick.py is exact-match-then-longest-prefix, so shell_policy substages (`stop_chain:*`) inherit the parent `stop` budget by default; only outliers like `stop_chain:holograph` need explicit entries.
